copy.title: Drift Wider — Other Side, No Arrows
copy.prompt: Same stunt, mirrored, no markers. Find the wider catch.
copy.game_context: 4-on-4 half-court shell. PG threatens a middle drive from the left slot. You are the right wing — the weak side. Your defender stunts off you, and both the hip arrow and the vision cone are gone.
copy.possession_setup: The ball is at the left slot. You are the right wing. Your defender (x3) is one pass away and steps toward the ball as the PG drives.
copy.decision_moment: x3 leans at the ball. No arrow, no cone — only the help pulse and his body. The wider spot on the right-wing arc opens.
copy.visible_cue: x3's weight has shifted inside and the help pulse flickers on him. The gap between you and the sideline is empty.
copy.best_read: Slide one step wider on the right-wing arc. The pass beats his recovery and the closeout is too long to contest.
copy.explanation_md: **Hardest version of the same read.** Other side, no markers. The stunt works identically on both wings — what changes is that you now have to feel it instead of seeing it drawn. Watch his weight. When it tips to the ball, slide wider. If you can make this read with no arrows on either side, you own it.

feedback.correct: Right read. Mirrored, no arrows — you felt the stunt and slid wider. Clean kickout.
feedback.partial: Fade kept spacing — the wider wing was still the cleaner catch.
feedback.wrong: Read his weight, not your spot. When it shifts to the ball, drift wider.

self_review_checklist: Did I read his weight shift, not wait for a marker?
Did I move the instant he committed to the ball?
Did I drift along the arc, not into the paint?
Did I keep my feet ready for the catch?
acceptable_reads: Fade to the corner — keeps spacing but trades the clean wing shot for a contested corner three.
bad_reads: Stand still — he recovers before the pass.
Cut into the paint — runs into the low man's wall.

drift_wider_on_arc.label: Slide one step wider on the wing arc.
drift_wider_on_arc.feedback_text: Best read. You felt the stunt off his weight alone. The kickout was clean.

fade_to_corner.label: Fade down to the corner.
fade_to_corner.feedback_text: Okay. Corner relocation kept spacing, but the wider wing was the cleaner shot.
fade_to_corner.partial_feedback_text: Fading kept spacing — the wider wing was the cleaner shot.

stand_in_wing.label: Hold the wing and call for the ball.
stand_in_wing.feedback_text: Not yet. His stunt only buys a half-second. Standing still lets him recover.

cut_into_paint.label: Cut into the paint to fill the rim.
cut_into_paint.feedback_text: Not yet. The low man is already there. Widen the kickout instead.
